scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-confirmed against the live Document360 docs site's per-endpoint embedded OpenAPI fragments (https://docs.thrivelearning.com/apidocs/*.md, fetched 2026-07-03; the metadata.json docs_url this bundle inherited, https://developers.thrivelearning.com/, does not resolve at all -- DNS failure -- and has been corrected to the real docs host). IMPORTANT DISCOVERY: the legacy connector's 3 endpoints (GET https://api.thrivelearning.com/{users,content,completions}, an 'items' envelope, page/limit pagination) do not appear anywhere in the real, currently-documented Thrive Public API at all -- the real API lives at https://public.api.learn.link (v1 data reads under /rest/v1, v2 user-lifecycle writes under /rest/v2), uses page/perPage pagination and a results/pagination envelope (or a bare array for a few endpoints), and its /users, /contents, /learning/completions resources have entirely different field shapes than the legacy stub. The 3 original streams are kept UNCHANGED for legacy parity; every new Pass B READ stream targets the real, currently-documented API on its own real host via a literal absolute-URL stream.path (conventions.md's squarespace/defillama absolute-URL precedent -- hardcoded, not config-templated, since conformance's synthetic per-property config value would otherwise corrupt a templated host into a bogus relative path, see the write-endpoints note below), each declaring a per-stream conformance.skip_dynamic marker (the engine resolves an absolute stream.path as an already-absolute target and never substitutes the replay server's origin for it). 13 new read streams: activities, contents_v1, learning_completions, assignments, assignment_enrolments, audiences, audience_members, audience_managers, tags, cpd_categories, cpd_entries, cpd_requirements, skill_levels. NO new write actions are implemented (capabilities.write stays false): unlike StreamSpec, WriteAction has no per-action or bundle-scoped conformance.skip_dynamic escape hatch in this engine version, and checkWriteRequestShape always executes engine.Write for real against a local capture server built from bundle.HTTP.URL -- but a write action whose OWN path is itself an absolute URL (the only way to target a genuinely different host than base.url, the same mechanism the read streams use) ignores that capture server's origin entirely and dials the REAL internet address baked into the literal path, live, in-CI, every conformance run (verified: a first attempt produced real 401s against https://public.api.learn.link after a 109-second real network round trip). This is a genuine, verified ENGINE_GAP -- ExecuteWrite/checkWriteRequestShape need a WriteAction-level (or bundle-level, mirroring MetadataConformanceMarker) skip_dynamic marker before an absolute-URL write action can be declared safely; documented here rather than shipped as an unverifiable, CI-network-dependent write path.

an aggregate per-user CPD-minutes-logged summary over an arbitrary caller-chosen date range, not a discrete syncable entity -- the documented CPDUserSummary schema has no unique per-record identifier at all (userId alone is not unique across different query date ranges)

this bundle's original legacy-parity users stream already covers Thrive user records (a different, simpler field shape); this real-API v1 users endpoint returns a richer, differently-shaped User object under a results/pagination envelope, but adding a second users-shaped stream alongside the legacy-parity one would be a confusing duplicate of intent rather than a distinct resource -- users/{id} and users/ref/{ref} single-lookup variants are excluded for the identical reason
